Human.description: Humans are the baseline metatype and have balanced natural attribute limits. Their principal mechanical advantage is a higher starting and maximum Edge rating, reflecting the greater share of luck available to human characters.

Elf.description: Elves are generally taller and more slender than humans, with pointed ears, exceptional natural Agility and Charisma, low-light vision, and exceptionally long lifespans. In Sixth World society they are often stereotyped as graceful, attractive, and aloof.

Dwarf.description: Dwarfs are shorter and stockier than humans, with greater natural Body, Strength, and Willpower. They are unusually resilient against pathogens and toxins and possess thermographic vision, though their smaller stature often makes human-sized environments inconvenient and increases Lifestyle costs.

Ork.description: Orks are larger and more powerfully built than humans, with prominent tusks, increased natural Body and Strength, and low-light vision. They have lower natural limits for Logic and Charisma and shorter average lifespans, while social prejudice frequently subjects them to stereotypes of aggression and brutality.

Troll.description: Trolls are the largest and physically most powerful of the five core metatypes. They possess exceptional natural Body and Strength, thermographic vision, extended Reach, and dermal armor. Their size also results in lower natural limits for several attributes and doubled Lifestyle costs because housing, equipment, and other necessities must be adapted to their massive frames.

category.Metatypes:
Metatypes
The Shadowrun 5e Core Rulebook provides five player-character metatypes: Human, Elf, Dwarf, Ork, and Troll. Each metatype has its own racial minimums and natural maximums for attributes, as well as racial traits and, in some cases, additional Lifestyle costs.

Attributes
Characters begin at their metatype's listed racial minimums at no Attribute Point cost. Each Attribute Point spent during character creation raises one Mental or Physical attribute by 1. At character creation, only one Mental or Physical attribute may be at its natural maximum; special attributes are excluded from this restriction.

Special Attributes
Edge begins at the minimum listed for the metatype. Magic and Resonance begin at 0 unless granted through the Magic or Resonance priority selection. Special Attribute Points from the Metatype priority may be assigned to Edge and, where applicable, Magic or Resonance.

Priority Options
The value listed for each available priority is the number of Special Attribute Points granted by that Metatype priority selection.

racial_traits.Low-Light Vision:
Low-Light Vision
Allows the character to see clearly in low-light conditions, reducing or eliminating visibility penalties where appropriate.
racial_traits.Thermographic Vision:
Thermographic Vision
Allows the character to perceive heat patterns and temperature differences, aiding vision in darkness and other conditions where heat contrast is useful.
racial_traits.Pathogen and Toxin Resistance:
Pathogen and Toxin Resistance
Dwarfs receive a +2 dice pool modifier on tests made to resist pathogens and toxins.
racial_traits.Reach:
Reach
Trolls have +1 natural Reach. Reach affects melee combat by modifying Defense Tests according to the difference between opponents' Reach values.
racial_traits.Dermal Armor:
Dermal Armor
Trolls possess +1 natural armor from dermal deposits. This protection is inherent to the metatype.
racial_traits.Lifestyle Cost:
Lifestyle Cost
Dwarfs pay 20% more for Lifestyle costs, while trolls pay 100% more. These increases represent the additional expense of adapting living spaces, equipment, and services to their physical requirements.
